title: Trichilia roka
taxonomy: Trichilia roka is classified within the family Meliaceae, a group of plants commonly known as the mahogany family. The genus Trichilia belongs to the subfamily Trichilioideae, which is a distinct subgroup within the family. Trichilia roka is a member of the order Sapindales, a large and diverse order of plants that includes many other families. The species is native to Madagascar, adding to the diversity of plant life found on the island. Trichilia roka's classification highlights its unique position within the botanical kingdom.
uses: Trichilia roka is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional African medicine. Its bark is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In some regions, Trichilia roka is used as a treatment for snakebites and other venomous bites. More research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential applications.

intro: Trichilia roka is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Meliaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties, which can help treat conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Trichilia roka include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Trichilia roka is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to treat various ailments, making it a valuable resource in African folk medicine.
